conjectures.label: nested-endpoint
conjectures.current_result: For the locked full class without nonfactual potential-outcome support, sup Theta_I(P) is +infty whenever the policy has positive probability of selecting a nonfactual action. The support-completion endpoint is sharp only for the explicit support-restricted subclass. The conditionally exchangeable joint-kernel endpoint is sharp for the CE subclass, and the separated nested endpoint is only an outer bound there unless joint realizability is proved.
conjectures.approach: Constructed Q_M by keeping the observed margin fixed, setting factual outcomes to Y, setting all nonfactual policy-relevant outcomes to M, and using constant U so the MSM odds ratio equals one. Then rebuilt the support-restricted and CE results separately.
conjectures.key_gap: Sharpness of the separated nested endpoint on the multi-action CE subclass still requires joint realizability of the actionwise least-favorable tilts.

conjectures.label: noncommutation
conjectures.current_result: The explicit term-by-term pointwise band satisfies W_band >= W_sep >= theta_U^jnt inside the CE actionwise-bounding workflow. The proposed two-action, three-outcome, one-cell monotone-tilt class yields equality, not a strict gap.
conjectures.approach: Defined the lower and upper thresholdwise CDF envelopes as actionwise bounded-weight LPs and used the monotone upper-tail rearrangement to compare the band criterion with the separated generated-loss criterion.
conjectures.key_gap: A strict separated non-commutation witness, if it exists, must use a richer support where no common monotone vertex simultaneously attains all threshold and generated-loss optima.

conjectures.label: onestep-limit
conjectures.current_result: The one-step expansion is proved for the separated endpoint under explicit canonical LP threshold differentiability, local CVaR curvature, global threshold localization, and second-order nuisance rates. It is a statistical theorem for the observed-law separated functional.
conjectures.approach: Added a canonical LP differentiability assumption instead of deriving uniqueness from no-atoms, kept the canonical dual pseudo-outcome and propensity derivative, and used global localization before the local quadratic expansion.
conjectures.key_gap: A limit law for the exact joint-compatible endpoint theta_U^jnt(P) remains open because differentiability of the joint kernel program has not been established.

assumptions_live.name: locked full class without nonfactual support
assumptions_live.note: The absence of nonfactual upper support implies an infinite upper endpoint by the Q_M support-escape construction.

assumptions_live.name: all-potential-outcome support
assumptions_live.note: Needed for the finite Manski support-completion endpoints; it is not part of the locked identifying restrictions.

assumptions_live.name: conditional exchangeability
assumptions_live.note: Needed for the joint-kernel representation and for deriving unselected potential-outcome support from the observed selected support.

assumptions_live.name: joint latent compatibility
assumptions_live.note: Within the CE subclass, encoded by one simplex-valued latent treatment-probability vector p and selected-outcome moment equations.

assumptions_live.name: separated actionwise MSM endpoint
assumptions_live.note: Derived as a computable outer bound for the CE subclass, not as a full-class endpoint and not as generally sharp without joint realizability.

assumptions_live.name: pointwise band envelopes
assumptions_live.note: Now defined term-by-term through actionwise lower and upper CDF LPs, which pins down W_band.

assumptions_live.name: canonical LP threshold differentiability
assumptions_live.note: Inference-only regularity. No-atom alone is not enough because support gaps can make the dual pseudo-outcome selection-dependent.

assumptions_live.name: global threshold localization
assumptions_live.note: Inference-only regularity that moves the global empirical minimizer into the local quadratic expansion neighborhood.

assumptions_live.name: local CVaR smoothness and curvature
assumptions_live.note: Needed for first-order silence of eta selection; set-valued or kinked minimizers can give non-Gaussian limits.

assumptions_live.name: second-order nuisance-rate regime
assumptions_live.note: The one-step theorem assumes coherent first-stage estimation of e, lambda, h, T, dot T_e, and the conditional distribution object with second-order drift remainder.

tried_and_failed.what: Separated actionwise minimax proof of sharp nested endpoint.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: It optimized over independent actionwise weights and omitted the latent compatibility constraint sum_a e_a/r_a = 1, so it proved only a CE-subclass outer bound.

tried_and_failed.what: Treating the joint-kernel program H_Gamma(P) as a representation of the locked full class M_Gamma(P).
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: The identity E[p_a b(Y(a)) | X] = E[1{A=a} b(Y(a)) | X] requires conditional exchangeability; the locked MSM assumption only bounds p_a = Q(A=a | X,U).

tried_and_failed.what: Claiming point identification at Gamma=1 under the locked full class.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: Gamma=1 makes A independent of U given X but does not make A conditionally exchangeable with the potential-outcome vector.

tried_and_failed.what: Claiming the finite support-completion endpoint for the locked full class from observed support of Y.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: Observed support controls only Y(A). Without Y(a) support for nonfactual actions, nonfactual policy outcomes can be sent to arbitrarily large values.

tried_and_failed.what: Original fixed-eta nuisance expansion with a mean-normalized transformed loss.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: It left a first-order drift from estimating the MSM transform because the transformed loss was not the canonical derivative pseudo-outcome.

tried_and_failed.what: Original propensity handling in the one-step score.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: It omitted the derivative of the MSM operator with respect to e_a through ell_a and u_a.

tried_and_failed.what: Original definition of zeta_eta,a by conditional mean only.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: A conditional mean identity does not identify the pathwise derivative of the bounded-weight LP.

tried_and_failed.what: Original eta-selection argument from uniqueness and derivative straddling alone.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: That condition permits kinks and does not prevent a global empirical minimizer from selecting eta outside the local expansion neighborhood.

tried_and_failed.what: Threshold-selection proof using only local nuisance expansion near eta_*.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: The estimator minimizes over all eta in [y_L,y_U]; without global uniform control or separation, eta_hat need not enter the local neighborhood.

tried_and_failed.what: Using no-atom at one threshold as the only LP differentiability condition.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: No-atom does not rule out threshold intervals in support gaps, and the dual pseudo-outcome can be selection-dependent.

tried_and_failed.what: Leaving the pointwise CDF band as an opaque F between unnamed envelopes.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: The band comparison requires term-by-term lower and upper thresholdwise LP envelopes; otherwise W_band is ambiguous.

tried_and_failed.what: Stating headline theorems under long assumption ranges.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: Identification and band comparisons do not use iid sampling or inference smoothness, while the one-step theorem does not use consistency or the causal MSM restriction except for interpretation.

open_subgoals.goal: Decide sharpness of the separated nested endpoint on the conditionally exchangeable multi-action MSM subclass.
open_subgoals.what_is_needed: Either prove joint realizability of the active separated tilts or construct an explicit CE observable law where theta_U^jnt(P) < W_sep(P).

open_subgoals.goal: Find or rule out a strict separated non-commutation gap.
open_subgoals.what_is_needed: Analyze richer supports where the pointwise-band extremal CDF and separated generated-loss extremals cannot be represented by one common monotone vertex across eta.

open_subgoals.goal: Develop inference for the exact joint-compatible endpoint.
open_subgoals.what_is_needed: Establish directional or Hadamard differentiability of the joint kernel program defining theta_U^jnt(P), or derive the correct nonstandard limit if differentiability fails.

open_subgoals.goal: Characterize the lower endpoint of the locked full class when nonfactual outcomes are unbounded below.
open_subgoals.what_is_needed: The current robust-welfare target needs the upper endpoint, which is infinite. A full two-sided locked-class set would require a separate lower-tail analysis depending on tau, policy nonfactual mass, and the ambient lower support.

open_subgoals.goal: Assess whether potential-outcome support or conditional exchangeability should be promoted to locked identifying restrictions in a future theorem instance.
open_subgoals.what_is_needed: A design decision from the pipeline or paper scope: the current locked full class has an infinite upper endpoint, while the nonvacuous MSM-CVaR analysis lives in support-restricted or conditionally exchangeable subclasses.

round_summary: This round removes the invalid finite full-class endpoint claim and proves the locked full class has infinite upper endpoint without nonfactual support. It relocates support completion to an explicit support-restricted subclass, pins down the pointwise band envelopes term-by-term, and makes LP threshold differentiability an explicit inference-only condition. The separated sharpness, strict non-commutation, and exact joint-endpoint inference questions remain open.
